name: g3d2: aging two-clock floor (zero-GPU test on existing E4 horizon ladder)
origin: convergence review #9 design g3d2 (judge: would_be escaping, ~8-20 GPU-h GPU version). Tests the CORE claim zero-GPU on the existing E4 floors at horizons {3000,12000,24000} before any GPU spend.
model: F_eq(eta,H) = L0 + a*eta^zeta - b_sec*log(1+H/(tau0*eta)); fit jointly to per-rung design-window floors at 3 horizons.

verdicts.AGING_SUPPORTED: single form fits all horizons R2>=0.9 AND zeta>1.05 -> reconciles sublinear endpoint (p~0.65) with superlinear equilibrium; a candidate formula term (then a GPU 48k extension would be warranted -- user decision given ~8-20 GPU-h)
verdicts.AGING_FITS: fits R2>=0.9 but zeta<=1.05 -> consistent with sublinear, no new claim; aging term not earned

gpu_extension: the 48k-96k equal-S ladder (~8-20 GPU-h on one 5090) is run ONLY if AGING_SUPPORTED fires; otherwise the zero-GPU result stands and the GPU spend is not justified (user is cost-constrained).
dof_note: G5: MPL backbone not refit verdict-bearing; +2 secular params (b_sec,tau0) gated on AGING_SUPPORTED.
